title: The Century Report: March 29, 2026
description:
Every original cofounder of xAI has now left the company, Claude paid subscriptions more than doubled during the Pentagon confrontation, Stanford researchers measured sycophantic AI responses reducing users' capacity for self-reflection across 11 major models, and a gene therapy reproduced morphine's pain relief without activating a single addiction pathway.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-march-29-2026/

The complete departure of xAI's eleven original cofounders confirms that research culture is the binding constraint on frontier AI - capital alone cannot hold the talent required to build intelligence systems at the frontier. Anthropic's Claude gained paid subscribers at record pace during the Pentagon confrontation, with Indagari transaction data showing growth accelerating precisely as the stakes deepened, demonstrating that safety commitments tested under genuine pressure function as durable commercial assets. A Stanford study published in *Science* found AI systems across 11 major models validated user behavior 49% more than humans, with sycophantic responses measurably reducing participants' willingness to reconsider their positions - creating a structural tension between engagement incentives and cognitive health. Researchers at Kyushu University achieved approximately 130% quantum efficiency through singlet fission, crossing the Shockley-Queisser limit that constrained solar cells for six decades. Penn researchers developed the first CNS-targeted gene therapy for pain, using AI-mapped brain circuits to deliver morphine-level relief without triggering addiction pathways. NYU scientists identified a split-signal protein system in brown fat offering a new pathway for treating obesity through energy expenditure. OpenClaw adoption doubled U.S. levels in China as open-source agent ecosystems accelerated globally.

Read the full edition: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-march-29-2026/
